metadata.title: ALNRI analysis - market rents (Apartment List / Zillow / BLS New-Tenant) vs official CPI shelter: the ~1-year lag in CPI's largest component
metadata.frame: The ALNRI analysis: compare the Apartment List National Rent Index (ALNRI), Zillow Observed Rent Index (ZORI), and the BLS New Tenant Rent Index (NTRI / R-CPI-NTR) - all MARKET / new-lease rent gauges - against the OFFICIAL CPI shelter series (Rent of primary residence CUUR0000SEHA + Owners' Equivalent Rent). Shelter is ~1/3 of CPI; because BLS samples the FULL STOCK of existing leases (most reset only annually), official shelter LAGS market rents by ~12 months and misreports every turning point by ~a year. BLS itself built NTRI to show this. This is the concrete deepening of macro-official-data-integrity's CPI-methodology strand.
metadata.WARNING: EVIDENCE-GRADED. The LAG is a documented, BLS-acknowledged methodological feature - FACT, not manipulation. The observation that the direction of the lag error aligned with policy convenience at each phase is the analytical (contested) layer, labeled. Apartment List / Zillow are private series with their own scope/method caveats; cross-read with BLS NTRI (same microdata).
metadata.method: Apartment List Research (ALNRI), Zillow Research (ZORI methodology), BLS New Tenant Rent Index + R-CPI-NTR research series + Cleveland Fed/Richmond Fed working papers, FRED CUUR0000SEHA. Compiled from public research sources.

data_sources.ALNRI: https://www.apartmentlist.com/research/national-rent-data (Apartment List National Rent Index - new-lease median rents)
data_sources.ZORI: https://www.zillow.com/research/methodology-zori-repeat-rent-27092/ (Zillow Observed Rent Index - asking rents, repeat-rent, quality-controlled)
data_sources.NTRI: https://www.bls.gov/pir/new-tenant-rent.htm + https://www.bls.gov/cpi/research-series/r-cpi-ntr.htm (BLS New Tenant Rent Index - official microdata, turnover units only)
data_sources.analysis: https://arkominaresearch.substack.com/p/inflation-and-the-fed (paywalled; thesis: 2024 'resurging inflation' was substantially lagged shelter)

cross_refs: macro-official-data-integrity.json (this is the deep-dive on the CPI shelter strand)
macro-fed-trap-regional.md (the Fed acted on the lagged shelter print)
macro-cre-privatecredit.json (CRE/multifamily valuations tie to actual rents)

the_divergence.grade: STRONG (multiple independent series + BLS's own NTRI)
facts: Dec 2021: Apartment List National Rent Index (ALNRI) +18% YoY; official CPI rent showed ~3% at the SAME time - a ~15pp gap.
Zillow ZORI ~15.2% YoY (Nov 2021) / ~15% early 2022 vs official CPI rent ~5.5%.
ALNRI YoY peaked Nov 2021 and LED the peak in rent-CPI by ~16 months.

BLS New Tenant Rent Index (R-CPI-NTR) uses the SAME microdata as official CPI but keeps only TURNOVER units and dates the price change to when it occurred (not when surveyed) -> it LEADS official CPI rent by ~4 quarters (~1 year). (BLS's own series, confirming the lag.)
Mechanism: official CPI shelter measures what ALL renters pay this month; most leases reset only annually, so a market-rent shock enters the index slowly, smeared over ~12 months.

the_two_phase_bias.phase1_understatement_2021_2022: When market rents exploded (+15-18%), official CPI shelter rose only ~3-5.5%, so headline CPI UNDERSTATED true inflation while the Fed funds rate was still ~0% - i.e., the lag helped justify staying too loose too long ('transitory').
the_two_phase_bias.phase2_overstatement_2023_2025: When market rents cooled (ALNRI/ZORI flat-to-negative in 2023-24), official CPI shelter stayed ELEVATED for ~a year, propping up headline CPI - the lagged shelter was a large part of the 2024 'inflation is resurging' narrative used to keep policy restrictive (Arkomina thesis).
the_two_phase_bias.implication: The single most-weighted CPI component is structurally ~1 year stale, so it BIASES the headline in whichever direction is ~12 months out of date - and in 2021-25 that error ran in the policy-convenient direction at each phase (loose when rates were zero, then 'sticky' when cuts would otherwise have been forced). Whether convenient or merely coincidental is the contested layer; the lag and its sign are fact.

policy_citation_timeline.summary: #53 - a dated chronology of WHEN Fed policymakers cited which shelter framing (lagged official all-tenant CPI vs leading new-lease/market rents), and how the choice tracked the desired stance. Speech-page quotes are VERIFIED primaries; FOMC-presser quotes are flagged QUOTE-UNVERIFIED (primary PDFs not openable this pass). The asymmetry is the thesis; grades attached.

chronology.shelter_framing: inflation 'transitory', pointed to durable goods; surging market rents/home prices essentially ABSENT despite shelter ~1/3 of CPI
chronology.series_leaned_on: lagged official shelter (still low) - the convenient frame; leading market-rent surge unmentioned
chronology.grade: VERIFIED speech page (the omission is the data point, not a quote)

chronology.who: Gov. Christopher Waller ('The U.S. housing market')
chronology.shelter_framing: EARLIEST explicit Fed lag acknowledgment: CPI 'measures rents people are currently paying, under leases slow to reflect market conditions' while CoreLogic +12% / RealPage asking +15% had run ahead; cited research CPI rent would 'double in 2022'
chronology.series_leaned_on: contrasts lagged official vs LEADING market/asking rents - used the leading signal to argue inflation would RISE

chronology.shelter_framing: all-tenant CPI/PCE is 'the right target' but the new-lease series is 'very pro-cyclical ... rents went up much more than the CPI ... and now they're coming down faster'
chronology.series_leaned_on: distinguishes both; acknowledges LEADING new-lease rents already falling

chronology.shelter_framing: cleanest statement: 'Housing inflation tends to lag ... because of the slow rate at which the stock of rental leases turns over. The market rate on new leases is a timelier indicator.' New-lease ~20% 'falling sharply since about mid-year'; official would keep rising 'well into next year' then fall
chronology.series_leaned_on: both - leading new-lease (already down) used to forecast the still-rising lagged measure

chronology.shelter_framing: 'The slowing growth in rents for new leases over roughly the past year can be thought of as in the pipeline and will affect measured housing services inflation over the coming year.'
chronology.series_leaned_on: leading new-lease data as the forward disinflation signal

chronology.shelter_framing: 'lack of further progress' on inflation; shelter's slow decline a named reason confidence hadn't grown enough to cut - higher-for-longer
chronology.series_leaned_on: LAGGED official shelter (slow to fall) emphasized to justify restraint - even though market/new-lease rents had normalized by ~late 2023 (Cleveland NTRR near flat vs CPI shelter ~5.7% Q1-2024)
chronology.grade: QUOTE-UNVERIFIED for shelter specifics (CNBC summary + presser PDF)

chronology.shelter_framing: housing/services disinflation 'slow and sometimes bumpy', then 'come down steadily'

policy_citation_timeline.did_fed_name_the_index: No evidence Powell or an FOMC statement named the BLS New Tenant Rent Index (R-CPI-NTR) by label - his language ('new leases', 'rents charged to new tenants', 'pro-cyclical', 'in the pipeline') is the concept without the name. The research originated at the Fed's own Reserve Banks and is cited there: Cleveland Fed WP 22-38R (built the NTRR/ATRR feeding R-CPI-NTR) + EC 2024-17 + the Oct-16-2024 release (rent inflation elevated to ~mid-2026); Richmond Fed Macro Minute (Apr-4-2023); Boston Fed (2024); Minneapolis Fed (2024). (An SF Fed rent-lag paper was not confirmed.)
policy_citation_timeline.the_asymmetry: The record supports the thesis ASYMMETRICALLY: 2021 leaned on lagged-LOW shelter to support 'transitory' (downplay, by omission); 2022 used the LEADING new-lease data because it predicted future disinflation (useful); but by 2024 - with leading rents already normalized - Powell leaned on the LAGGED, still-high official measure ('lack of further progress', 'sluggish') to justify higher-for-longer. The Fed invoked whichever shelter framing fit the stance; the cleanest evidence sits in the gap between Reserve-Bank research (which flagged the lag in real time) and FOMC rhetoric.
policy_citation_timeline.grade: the lag + which series each statement relied on = FACT/labeled-from-verified-speeches; the 'convenient at each phase' read = contested-analytical (consistent with the_two_phase_bias); presser quotes flagged for primary re-verification
policy_citation_timeline.verification_note: Anchor any public quote on the four VERIFIED speech pages (powell 2021-08-27, waller 2022-03-24, powell 2022-11-30, powell 2023-08-25); confirm presser wording (esp. 2022-11-02 'pro-cyclical' and the 2024 'lack of further progress' shelter lines) against the FOMC press-conference PDFs before relying on exact words.

why_it_matters_fiscally: Shelter ~1/3 of CPI feeds COLA (CPI-W), TIPS, and bracket indexation (macro-official-data-integrity). A smoothed shelter series also avoids spikes that would have forced larger COLA/TIPS payouts at the 2021-22 peak. And it ties to the real economy: CRE/multifamily valuations and private-credit marks depend on ACTUAL rents (macro-cre-privatecredit), not the lagged CPI proxy - so the same lag that flatters the inflation print can mask stress in apartment/CRE collateral.

edges.notes: ALNRI +18% (Dec 2021) vs CPI rent ~3%; ALNRI YoY peak led rent-CPI peak by ~16 months. Market/new-lease vs full-stock.
ZORI ~15% (late 2021) vs CPI rent ~5.5%. Asking-rent repeat index.
BLS's OWN new-tenant series, same microdata, leads official CPI rent by ~4 quarters - BLS confirming the ~1yr lag in its headline shelter measure.
Shelter (rent + OER) ~1/3 of CPI; a ~1yr-lagged largest component drives the headline at every turning point.

assessment: The ALNRI analysis is the cleanest, BLS-confirmed instance of the data-integrity thesis. Three independent market-rent gauges - Apartment List (ALNRI), Zillow (ZORI), and BLS's OWN New Tenant Rent Index - all lead official CPI shelter by ~12-16 months because the official series samples the full stock of mostly-annual leases. Since shelter is ~1/3 of CPI, this guarantees the headline misreports inflation turning points by ~a year: it UNDERSTATED in 2021-22 (market rents +18% vs CPI +3%, while rates were ~0%) and then OVERSTATED in 2023-25 (cooling market rents vs sticky CPI shelter, feeding the 'resurging inflation' narrative that kept policy restrictive). The lag is documented fact - BLS built NTRI to expose it; the only contested part is whether the convenient SIGN of the error at each phase was design or coincidence. Either way, the operational lesson is exact: read ALNRI/ZORI/NTRI for the real shelter signal and treat official CPI shelter (CUUR0000SEHA/OER) as a ~1-year-stale, policy-relevant lagging print. This block deepens macro-official-data-integrity's CPI strand and connects to the gold lens (home -81% in gold) and CRE/private-credit collateral marks. Overlay; not used in the proofs.
